growth_records.strain: not specified (free-living unicellular marine diazotroph, radius ~1 um)
growth_records.cite: Inomura et al. A quantitative analysis of the direct and indirect costs of nitrogen fixation: a model based on Azotobacter vinelandii. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.97. [PMC5315487]
growth_records.snippet: We applied the cell flux model framework to a simulation of Crocosphaera in the dark (they fix nitrogen at night) with a growth rate of 0.23 per day (Grosskopf and LaRoche, 2012) and 100% vitality.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate cited/modeled input, not measured here. mu = 0.23 per day; mu_per_h = 0.23/24 = 0.00958 /h; doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.23 day = 3.013 day = 72.3 h. O2 = 186 uM stated. No medium composition, no numeric uptake/secretion flux in text (respiratory-protection fraction ~60% is a qualitative respiration split, not a specific rate).
